description_long: This all-terrain vehicle features a rugged, open-frame design with a visible tubular metal chassis and exposed mechanical components. It is equipped with four large, knobby tires designed for off-road traction, and the rear wheels are notably wider than the front, enhancing stability on uneven surfaces. The seating consists of two bucket-style seats with high backrests, and a prominent roll cage structure surrounds the passenger area for safety. The vehicle's engine and drivetrain are exposed, with a yellow engine cover and visible exhaust system mounted at the rear. The front end is equipped with a straight handlebar for steering, and the overall construction emphasizes durability and functionality for rough terrain. The color palette includes black, yellow, and metallic tones, with some areas showing signs of wear and use.
description: A rugged, open-frame all-terrain vehicle with four large knobby tires, two bucket seats, a roll cage, and a yellow engine cover. The design emphasizes off-road capability and occupant safety.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide rear tires, dual bucket seats, and the straight handlebar for steering.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the tubular frame, exposed engine components, and the roll cage structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the yellow engine cover, exhaust system, and the symmetry of the wheel arrangement.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with clear visibility of the frame, seats, and mechanical layout.
attribute_tire_tread_pattern: The tires have a knobby, blocky tread pattern designed for maximum grip on loose or uneven terrain, typical of all-terrain vehicles.
